content:
✔ What Can Be Sacrificed?
• Permanent Item Loss → A student must give up a rare item forever.
• Status Infliction → A student permanently gains a minor debuff.
• Class-Wide Burden → The reversal introduces a different consequence (ex: everyone loses a privilege).

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Ensures that no fate manipulation happens without major consequences.
✔ Makes players consider if reversing a bad fate is actually worth it.
✔ Adds a social dilemma—who is willing to suffer for the greater good?

📜 2. Fate Can Only Be Countered Once Per Class (or Three Times Total)

📜 Every class session, only one fate reversal can occur.

✔ Fate Reversal Limits:
• Once per class → Only one fate counter can happen in any given session.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 532 (chunk 532/555)
